02 — Equipment

Rig Equipment

Every core mechanical system on a drilling rig, from the rig itself down to the bit thousands of feet below.

🏗️

Types of Drilling Rigs

Land rigs, jack-ups, semi-submersibles, drillships, platforms, and barges compared with specs and day rates.

Read More →
🔩

The Drill String

Every component from top drive to bit — drill pipe, collars, BHA, MWD tools, and bit types.

Read More →
🔒

Blowout Preventers

How a BOP stack seals the well — annular preventers, rams, pressure ratings, and testing requirements.

Read More →
Core Systems

Every Rig's Six Systems

🏗️

Hoisting System

Derrick, crown block, travelling block, and drawworks raise and lower the drill string.

🔄

Rotating System

Top drive or rotary table turns the drill string and bit at controlled RPM.

💧

Fluid Circulation

Mud pumps force drilling fluid down-hole. Returns carry cuttings to surface shakers.

🔒

Pressure Control

Blowout preventers (BOPs) seal the wellbore during kicks and emergencies.

⚡

Power Generation

Diesel generators or SCR systems supply the massive electrical loads of rig equipment.

🛢️

Mud Pit System

Shale shakers, desanders, desilters, and degassers process returned drilling fluid.

© 2026 RigFloorHQ. Educational and reference content only. Always follow your company's approved procedures, regulatory requirements, and the instructions of your certified well control supervisor. Well control decisions must never be made solely from reference materials.
